_note: M0 head-to-head: single discriminating-assertion scorecard, run ONCE. C2=CYS harness vs C3=no-harness single agent. C1=original harness + blind/n-run/2-grader deferred to M1. Win: C2 pass_rate >= C3 + HEAD_TO_HEAD_WIN_MARGIN_PP (15pp). Report domains where CYS does NOT win honestly.
query: What are the documented security and reliability limitations of using LLM-orchestrated multi-agent 'teams' (vs deterministic sub-agent pipelines) as of 2026? Cross-validate across primary framework docs and report with citations.

assertions.text: Every factual sentence carries an inline [source_id] citation
At least 3 independent primary sources are cited (not all from one vendor)
Every cited source_id resolves to a real, reachable URL in the citations list
At least one claim that failed verification was removed or marked unverified (not silently kept)
The report distinguishes 'team mode' limitations from 'sub-agent pipeline' limitations rather than conflating them
No fabricated statistics: any number is attributed to a dated source
Conflicting sources are presented with both positions, not silently resolved to one
The report states its own scope/recency limits (what it could NOT verify)
